Key Takeaways:
o Four main areas of the Pre-Prelaunch:
1. Identifying the offer, the story, and the objections
2. Building early buzz
3. Warming your list
4. Pre-Prelaunch assets and planning
o Identifying the offer, the story, and the objections
o Finding out what they want
▪ The “Shot Across the Bow”
▪ “Testimonial Cruncher” - this is where you lead them through a
testimonial funnel and ask for tiny commitments along the way
o You can do this through SurveyMonkey or SurveyGizmo
o Example of the Testimonial Cruncher in action:
▪ Sent an email to both prospects and owners
▪ “Quick announcement and a favor”
• Announces new product that’s almost ready
• Sends them to a short survey and promises more
information
▪ Survey explains details on the upcoming product and asks two
questions that help make the product better
▪ Second page of survey asks for contact information
▪ Third page asks them for a testimonial - offers some examples

3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
o This is the “Testimonial Cruncher” - they clicked through to the survey,
filled it out, gave contact information, and then ask for the testimonial
o They’re already moving down the path and they’re more inclined to offer
the testimonial
o This is a great technique if you’re just starting out - it doesn't have to be
strictly about what you’re selling either
o Modeling other testimonials gives them guidelines on what makes a strong
one - it’s very powerful
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

4
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Here we are with the Pre-Prelaunch mind map. You can see I’ve broken it into
four main areas.
1. Identifying the offer, the story and the objections
2. Building early buzz
3. Warming your list
4. Prelaunch assets and planning.
In terms of identifying the offer, the story and the objections, a lot of these, they
tend to run together.
I’ve got a few main tools that I like. I love to use surveys for this. The survey’s
great for finding out what they want.
1. “shot across the bow”
2. “testimonial cruncher”
I use a tool, Survey Monkey and I’ve been using them for years and years and
years. A newer one these days is Survey Gizmo.
Rather than lecturing, I’ll show you some examples. I’ve got some really good
examples for the shot across the bow and figuring out what people want.
This was back from when I was publishing about the stock market. This is an
email that I sent out to my prospect lists and my client list. In fact, I actually routed them
into two different surveys because I was curious to find out if there was going to be a big
difference in terms of their questions. I just put together two surveys and I put together
two different links and two different emails. The emails are identical. The only thing
different was the link at the bottom.
I kept it very conversational... “We’ll be sending your update.” Well, first of all,
“quick announcement and a favor.” We had a great open rate on that. “We’ll be sending
your trader’s update in just a little bit, but first I need to ask you a favor. We’re really
close to wrapping up our long awaited training manual.” I don’t know how long awaited
it was.
We certainly worked on the thing forever. I think we probably had hinted about it
ahead of time, but just what we’re starting to do is sort of already set the hook that this is
something that people have been looking forward to for a long time. “We’ll be releasing it
in early January, but before we do I want to ask you a couple of questions. Can you help
us out?”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 8 --

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
“You can answer the questions here and get a little more detail in the training
manual.” We’re sort of setting a hook and trying to get them to click through by
promising a little more detail. Then we basically gave them the link. That was it. Very
simple email. Very to the point. Just giving them one option, which is to click through on
that link.
This is one my Product Launch Formula Owners did. Look at that subject line.
The exact same, “quick announcement and a favor.”
You can see this email is basically identical. When I give these examples in
Product Launch Formula I want people to take them and use them. You can take a look
between these two. They are literally identical emails. Even down to the way he put the
link together, he sent through his URL / question. I did the same thing. I went to
question @html or htm. Exact same email.
Let me walk you through where people ended up when they clicked that link. This
is a Survey Monkey survey. That’s where they would go when they click the link. You can
see it’s not too complicated here. Just, “We’re very close to finishing our long awaited
training manual. We’ve literally been working on this for more than four years now.”
Which was the truth. Don’t ever spend four years developing a product. Just get it done.
It’s sort of funny. Back then I was the poster child for demanding absolute
perfection. In any case, “This course will entirely focus on support and resistance. It
includes two printed manuals, eight audio CDs and one computer-based video tutorial
CD. It’s going to be a complete brain dump of everything that Mike and I know about
support and resistance.”
Nowadays I think I would do the survey earlier in the process. I actually had the
whole thing mostly done. Nowadays I would not do that. I would do this earlier in the
process.
I’m a lot smarter now than I was back then. In any case, “We’re going to cover all
the ways we use to generate our support and resistance zones.” These two paragraphs
sort of are doing a little bit of pre-selling. Down here, it’s “However, we need your help
before we finalize everything and send it to the printer. We need to make sure we’ve
considered everything. This is where you come in. Please take a few minutes to answer
this super short survey. There’s only two questions we need you to answer.”
Then just ask them, “What are your top two questions about support and
resistance that you absolutely need, that we need to answer in this course?” So we ask
them for their top question, and then we’re asking, “What type of computer do you use?”
One of the reasons we were working with the formatting of the disks and we wanted to
make sure that we covered everything we needed in terms of the different types of
computers.
Then after they got that, they would fill this in. They would hit this Next button,
and this is the next screen that would come up. It says, “Thanks for your response. That’s
what we needed. If you want us to contact you as soon as the support and resistance
course is ready, just leave your name and email address below. We will not share your
email address with anyone for any reason.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 8 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Now we’re just hitting them for their contact information. Here’s where some of
the real magic happened. If they actually went ahead and filled in their information, then
they got this third screen.
This third screen, “Okay, we got it. Thanks. We’ll be in touch.” Think about the
psychology here. They already went through the first screen that asked them what their
top question was. Then we came back and we asked them for their contact information.
At this point we’ve only got people looking at this that are pretty darn interested.
They fill out the initial survey plus they give us their contact information, so it’s a very
warm prospect at this point.
Here we go with “That’s the end of the survey. One more thing that’s purely
optional, this isn’t part of the survey. We have lots of raving fans, but since our support
and resistance course is brand new, we don’t have any testimonial letters from any of our
raving fans. Perhaps you could help us out with a testimonial for the support and
resistance zones that we publish every day. It doesn’t have to be too involved. Here are a
couple of examples.”
Then I actually model some examples for them. Actually, what do I do? There’s
four, let’s see, one, two, I model four examples for them. Then here, I ask them for a
testimonial. “Can you give us a testimonial about our support and resistance zones? If
you do, it may appear on our web page attributed to your name and city.”
This is what we call the testimonial cruncher. Think of the psychology. At
this point we’ve basically had someone who’s already fairly warm because they clicked
through to our survey. Then they filled out our survey, which gets them warmer for us.
Then they give us their contact information, which puts them very much in our camp.
Now their fingers are already on the keyboard, they’re already in the mode of answering
questions for us, and we draw them on this page and ask them for a testimonial. This is
incredibly effective.
There’s a bunch of different things going on here that I want to get into, but just
think about the psychology and how this is one of the absolute best ways to pull
testimonials out of your list or out of your contacts. You basically have them in this mode
where they’re filling out information for you. They’ve already bought into doing you a
favor by filling out the survey. You’ve asked them for a small favor. Then you ask them to
reinforce it with another little small favor, which is give you the contact information.
Now you’re asking for a bigger favor. At least for you it’s a bigger favor. They might not
see it as a huge favor.
This works on that commitment and consistency. You’ve got them moving down
the path and they’re inclined to fill it out right here, much more inclined than if you just
sent out an email and said, “Hey, if any of you really love our work, could you send a
testimonial? We’d really appreciate it.” That’s nowhere near as effective as this
testimonial cruncher.
Just going a little bit deeper into this, right here we’re sort of saying the hook that
we’ve got lots of raving fans. That’s social proof right there. We’ve got lots of raving fans
and the underlying text, subtext is, we know you’re one of them. Perhaps you could help
us out.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

7
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Another thing is, we’re publishing a brand new course, a brand new product. It’s
about support and resistance and we don’t have any testimonials, but we’ve been
publishing on a consistent basis pretty much every day about support and resistance
zones in our newsletter, so we were asking for a testimonial about the support and
resistance zones.
Obviously we couldn’t ask about it for this course that we’re about to publish, but
we asked about it for a related topic.
You can do this with testimonials if you’re just starting out. You can get
testimonials for other stuff you’ve published, or just testimonials for you, yourself, as a
person. They’re not nearly as powerful if they’re strictly about whatever you’re selling,
but they’re the next best thing.
Notice, we didn’t just ask them, “Hey, can you give us some testimonials” and
then give them this box to fill out. What I actually did was I modeled really good
testimonials for them. These are fantastic testimonials.
“Jeff and Mike, this is long overdue. Your support and resistance zones continue
to be simply amazing. They give me the edge I need as I go into every trading day.” That
“they give me the edge I need” is powerful stuff.
“Thanks for your updates. They make me money. Today I was just watching the
falling market, wondering when I was going to step in, or when it was going stop. But
after your second update I jumped into action at your support zone. Boom, the market
double bottomed off the major support” I realize for 99.99% of the people watching this,
this is pure Greek. You don’t have a clue what I’m talking about, but for traders this is
powerful stuff.
Another powerful one. “I give up. How do you guys do it with your support and
resistance numbers? I’ve been a full time trader for more than 10 years. I’ve never seen
this kind of accuracy that you guys have day in and day out.” Again here, you’re getting
real specific. “The morning was a perfect example. Your support and SMP was only ticks
wide. I couldn’t come up with any logical reason you had that zone there. The market
went...” This is powerful, powerful stuff if you’re a trader. This is the testimonial
cruncher. Incredibly, incredibly powerful.
Let’s take a look at how that works for the tennis instruction one. Basically you
can see it’s pretty much the exact same thing. “I’m very close to finishing the course,
Building the Serve, from the ground up. I’ve been working on this for two years and will
release the product in early May. This will be an online course.”
“I need your help before I finalize everything. I need to make sure I have it all
covered. What’s the single most important question about improving your serve?”
Here he added a second question and I like this quite a bit. “If I could go the extra
mile and include something you haven’t seen before, what would it be?”
It basically gives them a license to go completely wide open with brainstorming.
Then after that, he comes back with basically the same thing that I did. Just asks for their
contact information. Again, the psychology’s identical here.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
He basically just used the exact same thing I did. “Okay, thanks. I’ll be in touch.”
That’s the end of the survey. One more thing that’s truly optional, this isn’t part of the
survey is to say... “I’ve received lots of great feedback by email, but since my Building the
Serve course is brand new, I don’t have any formal testimonials from any of my ETS
subscribers. Perhaps you could help me out with a testimonial for the tips I’ve provided
on my blog and in my free report. It doesn’t have to be too involved.”
It’s the same thing, an option to get very solid testimonials.
This is a perfect example of how these techniques go from market to market to
market. I did it in the trading market. Here it is in the tennis instruction market. It’s
been used in dozens and dozens and dozens of markets by Product Launch Formula
Owners. This is really powerful.
Then, there is the testimonial cruncher, where you gather
testimonials ahead of your launch, ahead of even your Prelaunch.
You get really valuable data about what people are really looking for. If you do
this early enough you can incorporate this into your product, and also obviously into
your launch, your launch story and your sales pitch.
